creator: zdbs@lif.de
text:
Note: might be duplicate of http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/124501

last lines from portmaster:

checking for LIBOIL... yes
configure: Using /usr/local/lib/gstreamer-0.10 as the plugin install location
checking to see if compiler understands ... yes
checking to see if compiler understands -Wall... yes
configure: set ERROR_CFLAGS to -Wall
checking host system type... (cached) amd64-portbld-freebsd7.0
source: not found
configure: Using ffmpeg revision 
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
===>  Script "configure" failed unexpectedly.
Please report the problem to multimedia@FreeBSD.org [maintainer] and attach
the
"/usr/tmp/usr/ports/multimedia/gstreamer-ffmpeg/work/gst-ffmpeg-0.10.4/config.log"

COMMENT:
The configure script, while invoked as "#!/bin/sh", incorrectly assumes that /bin/sh is the same as bash in that it calls the command "source".
IMVHO this is a gross LinSux-ism.

How-To-Repeat: Call portmaster gstreamer-ffmpeg-0.10.4
